programme.title: El señor de los cielos
programme.desc: Aurelio le tiende una trampa al Turco y lo amenaza, pero no recibe respuestas sobre el dinero. Después de mucho buscar a Leo, Ignacio Miravalle la encuentra y jura ayudarla a salir de la cárcel.

programme.title: El señor de los cielos
programme.desc: Mónica sugiere que Aurelio negocie su propia mercancía y la divida con los colombianos. Doña Alba se interpone cuando la idea separa a los hermanos Casillas.

programme.title: El señor de los cielos
programme.desc: Todos creen que Aurelio Casillas ha muerto en una sala de operaciones. Sin embargo, la muerte que tanto impactó al mundo del narco mexicano es, en realidad, una mentira de quien busca venganza.

programme.title: El señor de los cielos
programme.desc: Aurelio captura al Turco y lo tortura para descubrir dónde está el dinero que le robó. Mientras, el Chema Venegas, desconfiando de los Casillas, ordena a Tijeras secuestrar a doña Alba.

programme.title: El señor de los cielos
programme.desc: Después de un tiroteo, Chacorta y sus hombres rescatan a doña Alba. El Chema se obsesiona con conocer la verdad sobre la muerte de Aurelio, y pide la lista de doctores que lo operaron.

programme.title: El señor de los cielos
programme.desc: Chema les ofrece refugio al Turco y la familia de Aurelio en una de sus casas de Los Ángeles para tenderles una trampa. Mónica tiene que matar al cocinero para poder escapar.

programme.title: El señor de los cielos
programme.desc: Mónica vuelve a casa y se encuentra con noticias impactantes. Tras una llamada de su hija Rutila, Aurelio recorre Los Ángeles en busca del Turco y su familia.

programme.title: Caso cerrado

programme.title: Caso cerrado

programme.title: Caso cerrado

programme.title: Una familia de diez
programme.desc: Una supervisora visita la casa de los López para saber si la Nena aspira a una plaza como maestra. Para pasar la revisión, la familia debe fingir que son otras personas.

programme.title: Una familia de diez
programme.desc: Justito se da cuenta de que Victoria aparenta ser alguien diferente para agradarle a su novio Bobby. Plácido está decidido a pedirle un aumento de sueldo a su jefa.

programme.title: Una familia de diez
programme.desc: Pierre sale de la cárcel y le pide a la familia López que le ayude a ganar un concurso de modas. Además, Plácido tendrá que entretener a los amigos de su jefa, mientras Justito y Victoria disimulan que el elevador ya funciona.

programme.title: Una familia de diez
programme.desc: Es el cumpleaños de Victoria y de Justito así que los López quieren festejarlos, por lo que deben ingeniárselas ya que no tienen dinero para hacer la fiesta, hasta que Plutarco llega con una sorpresa en su portafolio.

programme.title: Rebelde
programme.desc: Mía se enoja al ver llegar a Sol y a su amiga a los ensayos de RBD. El enojo la hizo decidir terminar con el grupo. Llega el nuevo director.

programme.title: Rebelde
programme.desc: El nuevo director quiere que se lo respete y que mejor que ponerse contra los alumnos e imponerse cortándole el cabello a Santos, lo que hace que todos lo odien aún más.

programme.title: Rebelde
programme.desc: Mia dona parte de su ropa a los niños pobres, pero el director Font aprovecha su generosidad para quedar bien. Mia descubre que Sol y Miguel provocaron que regalara su ropa nueva. León obliga a Diego a firmar una acusación en contra de su madre.

programme.title: Rebelde
programme.desc: Lola saluda muy efusiva a Lupita. Font sorprende a Sol insinuándosele al profesor Gonzalo Alicia encuentra en el escritorio de Font fotografías de varias adolescentes Sol golpea a Mia con un balón Sol se enfurece al ver a Mia con Miguel.

programme.title: Rebelde
programme.desc: Roberta le dice a Mia y a Miguel que le preocupa el futuro de RBD. Hilda y Alicia sospechan que Font no es una persona honesta. Teo le comenta a José que la quiere pero siente que lo asfixia. Tomás le dice a Roger que le enseñará algo de Roberta.

programme.title: Rebelde
programme.desc: Tomás pretende mostrarle a Roger una grabación, pero José le roba el cassette Mia y Miguel planean que Roberta y Diego se reconcilien Roberta se entera de que Roger está enfermo Roger finge un ataque al corazón cuando Roberta termina con él.

programme.title: Rebelde
programme.desc: Font acepta que Santos realice un documental del colegio Sol logra que despidan a Mayra por recriminarle su acoso al profesor Gonzalo los alumnos se quejan de las injusticias del director Lola obliga a Sol a pedirle perdón a Lupita.

programme.title: Rebelde
programme.desc: El director les anuncia a los estudiantes que el profesor Gonzalo ha sido despedido del colegio por traspasar los vínculos entre docente y estudiante.

programme.title: Pasión
programme.desc: Ofelia le dice al sacerdote la verdad sobre Santiago como secreto de confesión. Alberto visita a Gaspar para preguntar por Ricardo. Lisabeta busca cómo arruinar la boda de Ricardo y Camila.

programme.title: Pasión
programme.desc: Inés le dice a Santiago que ya no se quiere casar. Ricardo y Mario buscan pruebas para inculpar a Jorge. Camila pide la compra de la concesión.

programme.title: Esmeralda
programme.desc: José Armando ve como única opción para estar juntos que, cuando el bebé nazca, lo den en adopción, pero Esmeralda no está de acuerdo y le exige que se vaya. Aunque Blanca quiere luchar por su hija, al llegar al jacal tendrá una sorpresa.

programme.title: Esmeralda
programme.desc: Esmeralda y Dominga llegan a la ciudad para atender su embarazo. Fátima no perderá más tiempo y procurará que el matrimonio sea anulado para que Graciela se case con José Armando. En tanto, Blanca podría encontrar a su hija muy pronto.

programme.title: Esmeralda
programme.desc: Gracias a sor Piedad, Dominga y Esmeralda consiguen un lugar donde quedarse. Adrián podría empezar a olvidar a Graciela en la fiesta del santo patrono del pueblo. Blanca, Crisanta y Fátima llevan ropa al asilo, sin imaginar qué es lo que encontrarán.

programme.title: Esmeralda
programme.desc: A pesar de conocer el paradero de Esmeralda, Rodolfo insiste en anular el matrimonio. Mientras, José Armando continúa en su egoísmo e indecisión, sin ir a su encuentro. Se pone en duda si su amor es tan fuerte como aseguraba.

programme.title: Esmeralda
programme.desc: A pesar de buscar a su esposa, José Armando no comparte el pensamiento de Esmeralda sobre Lucio. Crisanta enfrenta a Fátima y le reclama por las mentiras que dijo sobre el amor de Graciela hacia su sobrino. Rodolfo ha empezado el trámite de divorcio.

programme.title: Mañana es para siempre: Estrategias
programme.desc: Fernanda le cuenta a Margarita que Eduardo se suicidó. Eduardo teme que los Elizalde descubran su verdadera identidad. Santiago y Aurora deciden casarse. Bárbara se alegra al saber que la empresa de Gonzalo está en quiebra.

programme.title: Como dice el dicho

programme.title: Como dice el dicho
programme.desc: Rosenda apoya a su hijo Luciano para cumplir su sueño de ser un gran músico, pero al casarse con Pedro, éste la domina y obliga al muchacho a abandonar la casa.

programme.title: Seun Kuti
programme.desc: Seun Anikulapo Kuti possesses the grace, energy and fury of his father, the legendary artist Fela Kuti. With the musicians of Fela Kuti's legendary band Egypt 80, Seaun Kuti brings the most original incarnation of Afrobeat to life. Digging deep into different African traditions, Seun has brought his own touch to the music by reflecting the difficulties and cultures of the continent. Three-quarters of Egypt 80's current line-up is composed of musicians who didn't only play with Fela Kuti, but often were arrested and harassed alongside the founder of the Afrobeat.

programme.title: Paul Desmond
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Wayne Shorter
programme.sub-title: Wayne Shorter Quintet - Estival Jazz Lugano
programme.desc: Estival Jazz Lugano is an annual, open-air music festival that takes place over five days in the picturesque city of Lugano, Switzerland. Since 1979, this musical Mecca has been a must-attend event for all jazz and world music fans worldwide. In July 1996, renowned American saxophonist Wayne Shorter and his quintet performed at Estival Jazz Lugano. Together with pianist Jim Beard, guitarist David Gilmore, bassist Alphonso Johnson, and drummer Rodney van Holmes, Shorter shows he is still at his best.

programme.title: John Coltrane
programme.desc: Explore the music of one of the most influential and innovative saxophonists in jazz history: John Coltrane! Immerse yourself in this collection of remarkable performances from the early 1960s that showcase Coltrane's unparalleled artistry and unique sound during a transformative period in the artist's career. Recorded in various European cities, Coltrane is seen playing the songs “Autumn Leaves”, “Hackensack”, “My Favorite Things”, “I Want To Talk About You”, “Vigil”, and “Naima”. Tune in and experience why the music of John Coltrane has touched the lives of so many!

programme.title: Jo Jones & Milt Buckner
programme.sub-title: Jo Jones & Milt Buckner - Radio Télévision Suisse
programme.desc: In early September 1969, the duo of American drummer ‘Papa' Jo Jones (1911–1985) and American organist Milt Buckner (1915–1977) played a string of jazz concerts in Switzerland. Jo Jones, who pioneered playing timekeeping rhythms on a cymbal rather than the bass drum, became renowned in the 1930s in the Count Basie Orchestra. Milt Buckner rose to prominence as a pianist in the Lionel Hampton Orchestra in the 1940s, yet devoted himself to the organ in the 1950s. Swiss actor Jean Landier welcomed the two musicians to the Radio Télévision Suisse program ‘Variétés: Jasons Jazz' for a short interview, followed by performances of ‘Hamp's Boogie Woogie', ‘Georgia Mae', ‘Tea For Two', and ‘Flying Home'.

programme.title: Dhafer Youssef
programme.desc: Jazz à Vienne is one of the world's most prestigious jazz festivals. Ever since 1981, it has attracted a mix of jazz legends and exciting newcomers, with many of them playing return engagements. Each year welcomes outstanding international stars to the historical Le Théâtre Antique to impress and inspire their audiences. In 2018, Tunisian composer Dhafer Youssef alternated between his hypnotic voice and masterful oud playing at Jazz à Vienne. His stunning abilities have to be heard to be believed. He is joined by bassist Matt Brewer and drummer Aaron Parks.

programme.title: Chucho Valdis
programme.desc: Legendary Cuban pianist Chucho Valdés brought his band ‘Jazz Batá 2' to the Malta Jazz Festival on July 19, 2019. The air was thick with anticipation as Valdés took to the stage, revisiting the small-group concept of his mythical 1972 Cuban album ‘Jazz Batá'. The batá repertoire – the deep classical music of West Africa – permeates Valdés' piano solos throughout the concert. Valdés's band consists of Dreiser Durruthy (Batas and vocals), Yaroldy Abreu (percussions and vocals), Abraham Mansfarroll (percussion), Ramón Vazquez Martirena-Bajo (bass guitar). They delivered a performance that reverberated through the historic island.

programme.title: B.B.King
programme.sub-title: B.B. King Orchestra - The North Sea Jazz
programme.desc: The North Sea Jazz Festival is the largest indoor music festival in the world, known globally as the event where the past, present and future of jazz are featured within three days. Next to a firm base of jazz as the festival's staple music genre, many others, such as blues, soul, funk, or hip hop, pass by. Back in 1979, legendary American blues guitarist B.B. King gave an unforgettable performance for the audience in The Hague. Born in 1925 in a small town in Mississippi, 'Lucille' (as he nicknamed his guitar) helped him escape a miserable existence picking cotton. He arrived in Memphis in the late 1940s, where his cousin Bukka White taught him the tricks of the blues. Soon he developed his own style. King's virtuoso, narrative solos with lots of vibrato, have been an inspiration for most (rock) guitarists after him.

programme.title: Bernard Haitink & Berliner Philharmoniker
programme.desc: Bernhard Haitink conducts the Berlin Philharmonic in a performance of Gustav Mahler's Symphony No. 1. Directed by Barry Gavin, this concert took place at the Berliner Philharmonie in 1992. Mahler was inspired by Jean Paul's novel Titan, in which an artistically gifted young man, driven by his failure to find his way in society, eventually commits suicide in despair. The genesis of this work was not easy for Mahler: he composed it between 1887 and 1888, at the time when he was conductor of the Leipzig opera. The first version of the work first took the form of a two-part symphonic poem and premiered in Budapest in 1898. However, the work was poorly received by the public and critics, which forced Mahler to revise it. The new version resulted in the wonderful Symphony No. 1 that we know today, an incredible achievement for such a young composer.

programme.title: Riccardo Chailly
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: David Bateson, Tuva Semmingsen, Cassandra Lemoine & Bjørn Fjæstad
programme.sub-title: Fantasymphony – A Concert of Fire and Magic
programme.desc: The Danish National Symphony Orchestra once again opens the gates to magical worlds with an enchanting concert program. Under the baton of German conductor Christian Schumann, the Danish National Symphony Orchestra, Danish National Concert Choir, and Danish National Junior Choir team up to perform music from the most popular fantasy movies, TV series and video games, including The Lord of The Rings, Game of Thrones, The Hobbit, The Witcher, and League of Legends. Featured soloists in this concert are Norwegian mezzo-soprano Tuva Semmingsen, Canadian-Portuguese soprano Cassandra Lemoine, Danish musician Bjørn Fjæstad, and British actor David Bateson. This performance was recorded at the DR Koncerthuset in Copenhagen, Denmark, in April 2023.

programme.title: Roberto Abbado, Filarmonica Arturo Toscanini, Coro del Teatro Regio di Parma & Eleonora Buratto
programme.desc: Roberto Abbado conducts the Filarmonica Arturo Toscanini, the Coro del Teatro Regio di Parma, and four vocal soloists in an outstanding performance of Giuseppe Verdi's Messa da Requiem, recorded at the Parco Ducale di Parma, Italy as part of Festival Verdi 2020. The soloists are soprano Eleonora Buratto, mezzosoprano Anita Rachvelishvili, tenor Giorgio Berrugi, and bass Roberto Tagliavini. When his fellow composer Gioachino Rossini died in 1868, Verdi proposed to compose a ‘Messa per Rossini' in his honor, to be written by himself and several other Italian composers. Verdi wrote the concluding movement, ‘Libera me'. When the premiere was cancelled, the project lay dormant. When Italian poet Alessandro Manzoni passed away a few years later, Verdi revisited his plan to compose a requiem – this time in honor of the poet he so greatly admired. As an opera composer, Verdi knew better than anyone how to infuse the work with drama, as is evident in the revised version of his ‘Libera me'. The famous, powerful ‘Dies Irae' especially stands out, depicting the horrors of the Last Judgement.

programme.title: Costanza Principe
programme.desc: Italian classical pianist Costanza Principe (1993) started playing the piano at age 6, making her public debut at age 7. Before long she would be performing as a soloist with numerous prestigious orchestras in Italy, the UK, France, and South America. Seen here in 2021 at one of Italy's oldest extant Renaissance theatres, the gorgeous Teatro Olimpico in the northern Italian town of Vicenza, Principe performs Johann Sebastian Bach's ‘Concerto italiano' and ‘Ciaccona', as transcribed for piano by Ferruccio Busoni.

programme.title: David Rabinovich, Apollo Ensemble, Renate Arends & Elvire Beekhuizen
programme.desc: David Rabinovich conducts the Apollo Ensemble ina performance of G. Ph. Telemann's Brockes Passion, TWV5:1. Soloists are Renate Arends and Elvire Beekhuizen (soprano), Franske van der Wiel and Nicola Wemyss (alto), Falco van Loon and Pablo Gregorian (tenor) and Michiel Meijer and Andrew Hopper (bass). Recorded at Oudshoornse Kerk, Alphen aan de Rijn, The Netherlands in 2019. The earth trembles and shakes, Mary cries, the apostles don't know what to do ... Barthold Heinrich Brockes wrote a masterly libretto in 1712 about the suffering of Jesus. He places the audience in the middle of the story, Telemann adds even more force to the drama of the suffering story with his emotional music, with an unusual and grand occupation at that time, even using natural horn. The eight singers sing the roles of the characters from the story such as Jesus, Mary, Peter and Caiaphas and also take care of the judgments. The result is an extremely lively, exciting performance that will stay with the audience for a long time.

programme.title: Flying Over Spanish Islands
programme.desc: In their first collaboration, cinematographer Stefan Zimmerman and Nature Relaxation™ producer David Huting present "Above the Spanish Islands." This 50-minute 4K aerial film captures Mallorca and the Canary Islands, set to ambient music by Diamans, Electus, and Michael St. Laurent.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland
programme.desc: "Flying Over Iceland: Part I" provides a soothing aerial journey through eastern Iceland's fjords and valleys, starting at sunrise in Brei_dalsvik. Enjoy views of mists, a waterfall, and the Brei_dals' river, set to nature sounds and music by The Cynic Project, showcasing East Iceland's beauty.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland 2
programme.desc: "Flying Over Iceland II: Summer in the Golden Circle" presents a serene aerial view of Iceland's south coast, starting at Skogafoss and moving to Vik, with black sand beaches, cliffs, and Vatnajökull glacier. Filmed in 2020, this 1-hour film features local wildlife and immersive music.

programme.title: Flying Over Kauai
programme.desc: "Flying Over Kauai" provides a soothing aerial journey over Hawaii's Garden Island, featuring the lush NaPali Coast, waterfalls, canyons, and beaches. Paired with new music by The Cynic Project and nature sounds, this 4K film offers a visually and sonically immersive experience.

programme.title: Flying Over Oahu
programme.desc: "Flying Over Oahu" offers a 97-minute aerial journey over Oahu's iconic coastlines with nature sounds and music by Alex Smith (The Cynic Project). From Waikiki Beach to Turtle Bay, enjoy breathtaking 4K landscapes, ending with a stunning Waikiki sunset.

programme.title: A Day in Paradise 1
programme.desc: A graciously flowing four hour long pure nature relaxation experience, "A Day In Paradise" immerses viewers in the sights and sounds of one of the world's most pure and picturesque tropical islands - for an entire day from sunrise, to sunset. Filmed in 2013 by David Huting, the tiny island is hidden off of the northeastern coast of the outer Fiji Islands (near Taveuni), and boasts a perfect white sandy beach, groves of palm trees, and a majestic lagoon that shelters the island from all sides. It has more turtle visitors than humans!

programme.title: East Bali (Music)
programme.desc: Relax to a serene soundtrack as you journey to spectacular beaches from Bali.

programme.title: Barracuda Lake
programme.desc: Immerse yourself in the calm green waters of this unique lake in the Philippines, interspersed by lush coasts.

programme.title: Playa Fronton: Dominican Republic
programme.desc: Feel the sand beneath your feet as you enjoy the soft lapping of waves on this tropical beach.

programme.title: Snake Island: Philippines
programme.desc: Fly away to paradise and explore the lush forests and turquoise waters of Snake Island, in the Philippines.

programme.title: Tumalog Falls: Philippines (Music)
programme.desc: Start your journey marveling at the cascading turquoise waters of Tumalog Falls, nestled amidst the lush forest and tropical foliage, and stay captivatingly thrilled by Aguinid Falls' multi-tiered rapids and unique rock formations.

programme.title: Peaceful Forest Walk: Quebec
programme.desc: A unique spot in the heart of the Laurentians: gaze over the peaceful lake as its smooth waves gently crawl to the shore.

programme.title: Eyes on the East—Part 1
programme.desc: Experience the vivid mosaic of Asian American and Pacific Islander heritage, where each artwork tells a story of the community's diversity and resilience. The collection is visual celebration capturing the essence of a vibrant cultural tapestry.

programme.title: Shades of Orange
programme.desc: Celebrating the warm and vibrant tones of orange, utilizing a variety of mediums to create a sense of energy, passion, and warmth.

programme.title: Autumn Hues—Part 1
programme.desc: Immerse yourself in a kaleidoscope of fall foliage, where trees dance in radiant shades and leaves gently cascade in the crisp breeze. This assortment of artworks celebrates the rich palette of warm hues, including burnt oranges, deep reds, and earthy browns.

programme.title: Tranquil Impressions—Part 2
programme.desc: Experience a serene and evocative art collection designed to provide a peaceful retreat from the chaos of daily life. This collection inspires calm, wonder, and awe, offering a soothing escape that captivates the soul and rejuvenates the spirit.

programme.title: After Hours—Part 1
programme.desc: A mesmerizing late night art palette and mood-setting experience that invites you to indulge your senses and explore the ethereal realm of nocturnal beauty. The accompanying playlist sounds a lot like a loft party after midnight: dark and dreamy.

programme.title: Hypnotic Visuals—Part 2
programme.desc: A motion artwork collection evoking the dreamlike haze of late-night thoughts, where surreal visuals flow freely. The evolving pieces reflect the introspective, mysterious atmosphere of the night, inviting viewers into an abstract, meditative space.

programme.title: Surreal Psychedelic—Part 2
programme.desc: Explore the boundaries of perception where the mind bends & reality warps, inviting you to transcend the ordinary and embrace the extraordinary. Electric guitar, wavy synths and lush keyboards create a tidal motion of sound. Surrealism turned sonic.
